name: Multi-Criteria Rule (AND / OR Logic)
summary: Show, hide, enable, or disable an element based on multiple field values using `scope: "#"` with a full JSON Schema condition
whenToUse: Hide a field when two or more conditions are met simultaneously (AND logic)
Show a section only when field A has value X AND field B has value Y
Show a field when ANY of several conditions is true (OR logic with anyOf)

options.help: This field only appears when you are employed AND in Alberta.
This field appears when you are retired OR self-employed.

notes: For AND logic: set `condition.scope` to `"#"` (root) and provide a JSON Schema with multiple `properties` and `required`
All listed properties must match for the condition to be true — this is AND logic
For OR logic: use `anyOf` inside the condition schema — the condition is true if ANY sub-schema matches
IMPORTANT: Include `required` in the condition schema to ensure fields must be present for the condition to match
You can combine AND and OR: `{ properties: { a: ... }, required: [...], anyOf: [...] }`
This works with all effects: SHOW, HIDE, ENABLE, DISABLE
For negation (NOT): wrap in `not: { properties: { ... }, required: [...] }` inside the condition schema
The single-field `condition.scope: "#/properties/fieldName"` pattern only checks ONE field — use `scope: "#"` for multi-field conditions
